% GENERATED by scripts/gen_blueprint.py — do not edit.
\chapter{The unified theorem: Conjecture 5.10 at $k = 3,4,5$}
\label{bp:ch_unified}

\section{The headline: every $k \in \{3,4,5\}$}

\begin{theorem}[\code{conjecture\_5\_10\_at\_345}]
  \label{res:conjecture_5_10_at_345}
  \uses{def:arc, def:backedge, def:kcritical, def:ltp, def:omegabar, def:subdel, def:tournament}
  \rocq{Digraph.applications.unified.conjecture_5_10_at_345}
  \rocqok

\begin{verbatim}
Theorem conjecture_5_10_at_345 (k : nat) : (3 <= k <= 5)%N ->
  forall N : nat, exists T : tournament, kcritical k T /\ (N < #|T|)%N.
\end{verbatim}
\href{https://github.com/LLM4Rocq/digraph-theory/blob/main/theories/applications/unified.v\#L66}{Source: \code{theories/applications/unified.v:66}}


\paragraph{Decoded.} For every $k$ with $3 \le k \le 5$ and every bound $N$ there exists a
$k$-$\bar\omega$-critical tournament on more than $N$ vertices: the
$k$-critical tournaments form an infinite family. (``$T$ is
$k$-critical'' unfolds per \ref{def:kcritical}: $\bar\omega(T) = k$
and $\bar\omega(T - v) = k - 1$ for every vertex $v$.)

\end{theorem}

\section{$k = 3$: infinitely many, and Question 5.9 fails}

\begin{theorem}[\code{conjecture\_5\_10\_at\_k3}]
  \label{res:conjecture_5_10_at_k3}
  \uses{def:arc, def:backedge, def:kcritical, def:ltp, def:omegabar, def:subdel, def:tournament}
  \rocq{Digraph.applications.unified.conjecture_5_10_at_k3}
  \rocqok

\begin{verbatim}
Theorem conjecture_5_10_at_k3 (N : nat) :
  exists T : tournament, kcritical 3 T /\ (N < #|T|)%N.
\end{verbatim}
\href{https://github.com/LLM4Rocq/digraph-theory/blob/main/theories/applications/unified.v\#L33}{Source: \code{theories/applications/unified.v:33}}


\paragraph{Decoded.} First statement: for every $N$ there is a 3-critical tournament on
more than $N$ vertices (the witness is $\mathrm{AC}_n$ itself,
$n = 2\max(2,N){+}3$). Second: moreover the witness can be chosen so
that \emph{its only subtournament with $\bar\omega \ge 3$ is the whole
tournament} --- formally, any vertex subset $S$ whose induced
subtournament has $\bar\omega \ge 3$ must be all of $V$
(\code{[set: T]} is the full set). Hence no function
$\ell(3)$ as in Question 5.9 can exist: witnesses have unbounded size
but no proper witnessing subtournament at all.

\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[\code{question\_5\_9\_fails\_at\_k3}]
  \label{res:question_5_9_fails_at_k3}
  \uses{def:arc, def:backedge, def:kcritical, def:ltp, def:omegabar, def:subdel, def:tournament}
  \rocq{Digraph.applications.unified.question_5_9_fails_at_k3}
  \rocqok

\begin{verbatim}
Theorem question_5_9_fails_at_k3 (L : nat) :
  exists T : tournament,
    [/\ kcritical 3 T, (L < #|T|)%N
      & forall S : {set T}, (3 <= ω̄(sub_tournament S))%N -> S = [set: T]].
\end{verbatim}
\href{https://github.com/LLM4Rocq/digraph-theory/blob/main/theories/applications/unified.v\#L47}{Source: \code{theories/applications/unified.v:47}}


\end{theorem}

  exists T : tournament, kcritical 4 T /\ (N < #|T|)%N.
\end{verbatim}
\href{https://github.com/LLM4Rocq/digraph-theory/blob/main/theories/applications/k4/k4_main.v\#L74}{Source: \code{theories/applications/k4/k4\_main.v:74}}


\paragraph{Decoded.} As for $k = 3$, with witness $\mathrm{AC}_n[C_3]$ on $3n$ vertices
(\ref{def:T4family}; the underlying values $\bar\omega = 4$,
$\bar\omega(T - v) = 3$ are \ref{res:omegabar_T4}, \ref{res:omegabar_T4_del}).

\end{theorem}
